title: The Wrong Branch
subtitle: 5 interleaved commits on main — sort them onto the right branches

briefing.narrative:
Five commits on main. Every single one is a feature commit. None belong here.

But it gets worse: they're interleaved. Three commits belong on feature-ui (they modify UI files), and two belong on feature-api (they modify API files). They're NOT in a convenient order — UI, API, UI, UI, API.

The Head Monk looks at the git log like a disappointed parent reading a report card. "You need to EXAMINE each commit. Read the log. Use git show if you must. Figure out which commits are UI work and which are API work."

"Then: create both feature branches, cherry-pick the right commits to each, and reset main back to where it belongs."

The cat has already sorted its yarn by color, texture, and philosophical significance. It finds your chaos offensive.
briefing.concept:
When commits are on the wrong branch, you need to:
1. Examine each commit with git log or git show to understand what it does
2. Create the correct branches
3. Cherry-pick each commit to the right branch
4. Reset the original branch to remove them

The key skill is INVESTIGATION — you must determine where each commit belongs before acting.
objectives: Examine the commits on main to determine which are UI and which are API
Create feature-ui branch and cherry-pick the 3 UI commits to it
Create feature-api branch and cherry-pick the 2 API commits to it
Reset main back to only the base commit
All feature files must exist on their correct branches

args.content:
UI: header component
logo: monastery.png
nav: home, about, contact

API: /users endpoint
GET: list all users
POST: create user

UI: sidebar navigation
links: dashboard, settings
collapsible: true

UI: footer component
copyright: monastery 2026
links: privacy, terms

API: /posts endpoint
GET: list posts
POST: create post
DELETE: remove post

hints.text: Use git log --oneline to see all commits. The header, sidebar, and footer are UI. The users-api and posts-api are API work. Cherry-pick by referencing HEAD~N.

1) git log --oneline to identify commits. 2) git branch feature-ui, git branch feature-api. 3) git checkout feature-ui, cherry-pick the 3 UI commits. 4) git checkout feature-api, cherry-pick the 2 API commits. 5) git checkout main, git reset --hard HEAD~5.
